docs.title: Glyph Engine
docs.text:
A code-first, cross-platform 2D game engine. C++17 core with Lua 5.4 scripting. Write your game once — deploy to Windows, macOS, Linux, Web (WebAssembly), iOS, and Android.

No GUI editor. No drag-and-drop. Your game lives in code.

docs.title: Design philosophy
docs.text:
• Code is the editor. Levels come from Tiled (.tmj JSON), sprites from Aseprite (.json + PNG). Everything else is C++ or Lua.
  • One codebase, every platform. Platform-specific code is isolated behind interfaces in src/platform/. Game code never touches #ifdef _WIN32.
  • Boring tech. SDL3, OpenGL 3.3 / GLES 3.0 / WebGL2, single-header libraries. No bleeding edge.
  • Y-down, pixel units. Origin at top-left. One world unit = one screen pixel at zoom 1.0. Matches Tiled, SDL, and screen coordinates.

docs.title: Quick example
docs.text:
#include <glyph/glyph.h>

class MyGame : public glyph::Game {
public:
    glyph::AppConfig configure() override {
        glyph::AppConfig c;
        c.title  = "My Game";
        c.width  = 1280;
        c.height = 720;
        return c;
    }

    void on_start() override {
        // load assets, create entities
    }

    void on_update(float dt) override {
        // game logic, input handling
    }

    void on_render(glyph::Renderer& r) override {
        r.clear(glyph::Color::rgba8(30, 30, 30));
        // draw calls
    }
};

GLYPH_MAIN(MyGame)

docs.title: Build
docs.text:
# Clone with submodules
git clone --recurse-submodules https://github.com/JMit-dev/glyph.git
cd glyph

# Configure and build (requires CMake 3.20+, Python 3, C++17 compiler)
cmake -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
cmake --build build -j

# Run the hello window sample
./build/samples/01_hello_window/01_hello_window

See Getting Started for a full walkthrough.

docs.title: Tech stack
docs.text:
Concern Library Window / input SDL3 3.2+ GL loader (desktop) glad2 Math glm 1.0+ (phase 5) Image loading stb_image (phase 6) Audio miniaudio (phase 9) ECS EnTT (phase 10) Scripting Lua 5.4 + sol2 (phase 16) JSON nlohmann/json (phase 12)

All dependencies are vendored as git submodules under external/.

docs.title: Current status
docs.text: The engine is in active development. See Build Progress for the current phase.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Tool Version Notes CMake 3.20+ Build system C++ compiler C++17 MSVC 2019+, GCC 9+, Clang 10+ Python 3.8+ Required by glad2 at CMake configure time Git any For submodule management

docs.title: Cloning
docs.text:
Always clone with --recurse-submodules to pull SDL3, glad2, and future dependencies:

git clone --recurse-submodules https://github.com/JMit-dev/glyph.git
cd glyph

If you already cloned without the flag:

git submodule update --init --recursive

docs.title: Building (Desktop)
docs.text:
# Debug build
cmake -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug
cmake --build build -j

# Release build
cmake -B build-release -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
cmake --build build-release -j

Binaries land in build/samples/<name>/.

docs.title: Running the samples
docs.text:
# Hello window — verifies GL context and textured quad
./build/samples/01_hello_window/01_hello_window      # Linux/macOS
build\samples\01_hello_window\Debug\01_hello_window.exe  # Windows

You should see a cornflower-blue window with a black-and-white checkerboard in the center.

docs.title: Writing your first game
docs.text:
1. Create a directory under samples/:

samples/my_game/
├── CMakeLists.txt
└── main.cpp

2. CMakeLists.txt:

glyph_add_executable(my_game main.cpp)

3. Register it in the root CMakeLists.txt:

if(GLYPH_BUILD_SAMPLES)
    add_subdirectory(samples/01_hello_window)
    add_subdirectory(samples/my_game)   # add this
endif()

4. main.cpp:

#include <glyph/glyph.h>

class MyGame : public glyph::Game {
public:
    glyph::AppConfig configure() override {
        glyph::AppConfig c;
        c.title = "My Game";
        return c;
    }

    void on_render(glyph::Renderer& r) override {
        r.clear(glyph::Color::rgba8(40, 40, 40));
    }
};

GLYPH_MAIN(MyGame)

5. Rebuild:

cmake --build build -j

docs.title: CMake options
docs.text: Option Default Description GLYPH_BUILD_SAMPLES ON Build the samples directory GLYPH_BUILD_TESTS OFF Build the test suite CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE Debug Debug, Release, RelWithDebInfo

docs.title: Platform compile definitions
docs.text:
These are automatically set by CMake and available in all engine and game source files:

Macro Platform GLYPH_PLATFORM_DESKTOP Windows, macOS, Linux GLYPH_PLATFORM_WEB Emscripten / WebAssembly GLYPH_PLATFORM_ANDROID Android NDK GLYPH_PLATFORM_IOS iOS

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
All public headers live under include/glyph/. Include the umbrella header to get everything:

#include <glyph/glyph.h>

Or include individual headers as needed:

#include <glyph/app.h>       // Game, AppConfig, GLYPH_MAIN
#include <glyph/scene.h>        // Scene, Entity
#include <glyph/components.h>   // Transform, Sprite, Velocity, ...
#include <glyph/sprite_sheet.h> // SpriteSheet, AnimClip
#include <glyph/math.h>      // vec2, mat4, Color, Rect, lerp
#include <glyph/camera.h>    // Camera, FitMode
#include <glyph/audio.h>     // Audio, Sound, Music, SoundHandle
#include <glyph/input.h>     // Input, Key, MouseButton
#include <glyph/time.h>      // Time, kFixedDt
#include <glyph/renderer.h>  // Renderer
#include <glyph/texture.h>   // Texture
#include <glyph/resources.h> // Resources

docs.title: Available modules
docs.text: Header Contents Since app.h Game, AppConfig, GLYPH_MAIN v0.1.0 scene.h Scene, Entity v0.10.0 components.h Transform, Sprite, Velocity, etc. v0.10.0 sprite_sheet.h SpriteSheet, AnimClip v0.12.0 audio.h Audio, Sound, Music, SoundHandle v0.9.0 math.h vec2, vec3, mat4, ivec2, Color, Rect, lerp v0.2.0 / v0.5.0 camera.h Camera, FitMode v0.5.0 input.h Input, Key, MouseButton v0.7.0 time.h Time, kFixedDt, kMaxAccum v0.8.0 renderer.h Renderer v0.2.0 texture.h Texture v0.3.0 resources.h Resources v0.6.0

docs.title: Coming in future phases
docs.text: Header Contents Phase tilemap.h Tiled TMJ loader 13 lua_api.h Lua scripting layer 16

docs.title: Audio
docs.text:
Header: #include <glyph/audio.h>

Thin wrapper over miniaudio. The audio device runs on its own thread internally — do not call any Audio methods from background threads.

Access via audio() inside any Game lifecycle hook. Obtain Sound and Music via Resources.

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
void on_start() override {
    res_.set_root("assets");
    shoot_sfx_ = res_.sound("shoot.wav");
    bgm_       = res_.music("theme.ogg");

    audio().play_music(bgm_, 1.0f);   // fade in over 1 second
}

void on_update(float dt) override {
    if (input().action_pressed("fire"))
        audio().play(shoot_sfx_, 0.8f);   // volume 0.8, fire-and-forget
}

void on_shutdown() override {
    audio().stop_music();
    shoot_sfx_.reset();
    bgm_.reset();
    res_.unload_unused();
}

docs.title: SoundHandle
docs.text:
Returned by Audio::play(). Discard for fire-and-forget; keep it to stop or adjust volume later.

glyph::SoundHandle h = audio().play(snd);
audio().set_volume(h, 0.5f);
audio().stop(h);

A handle is silently invalidated when the sound finishes — calling stop/set_volume on a finished handle is safe.

docs.title: Sound
docs.text: A short audio clip decoded fully into memory for low-latency, multi-instance playback. Obtained from Resources::sound(). Supported formats: WAV, OGG, MP3, FLAC (via miniaudio).

docs.title: Music
docs.text: A long audio track streamed from disk. Obtained from Resources::music(). Automatically looped when played via Audio::play_music().

docs.title: play(sound, volume, pitch, looped) → SoundHandle
docs.text:
Play a sound effect. Decodes the file to memory (MA_SOUND_FLAG_DECODE) on first play.

Parameter Default Description volume 1.0 Linear gain [0, 1] pitch 1.0 Playback speed scalar looped false Loop indefinitely

docs.title: stop(SoundHandle)
docs.text: Stop and release the sound instance immediately.

docs.title: set_volume(SoundHandle, float)
docs.text: Adjust volume of a playing sound.

docs.title: play_music(music, fade_in_seconds)
docs.text: Stream a music track. Replaces any currently playing music. Pass fade_in_seconds > 0 for a smooth start.

docs.title: stop_music(fade_out_seconds)
docs.text: Stop the music. fade_out_seconds = 0 stops immediately; > 0 fades out over that duration (music continues until next play_music or shutdown).

docs.title: master_volume(float)
docs.text: Global volume scalar applied to all sounds and music. Range [0, 1].

docs.title: Resources integration
docs.text:
glyph::Resources res_;
std::shared_ptr<glyph::Sound> shoot_;
std::shared_ptr<glyph::Music> bgm_;

void on_start() override {
    res_.set_root("assets");
    shoot_ = res_.sound("sfx/shoot.wav");
    bgm_   = res_.music("music/theme.ogg");
}

Resources::sound() and Resources::music() cache by resolved path and return shared_ptr handles.

docs.title: Thread safety
docs.text: All Audio methods must be called from the main thread. miniaudio manages its own audio callback thread internally; you never need to touch it.

docs.title: Camera
docs.text:
Header: #include <glyph/camera.h>

A 2D orthographic camera. Controls what region of world space is visible and at what zoom level. Pass to Renderer::set_camera() or modify Renderer::camera() directly before draw calls.

docs.title: Fields
docs.text: Field Type Default Description position vec2 {0, 0} World position at the centre of the view zoom float 1.0 >1 = zoomed in (things appear larger), <1 = zoomed out rotation float 0.0 Radians, clockwise positive (y-down convention) viewport_size vec2 {1280, 720} Logical resolution in world units. Updated by Renderer::set_viewport().

docs.title: view_projection() → mat4
docs.text:
Returns the combined orthographic × view matrix for the current camera state. The renderer calls this internally at begin_frame() — you rarely need it directly.

// Example: passing to a custom shader
mat4 vp = camera.view_projection();
glUniformMatrix4fv(loc, 1, GL_FALSE, glm::value_ptr(vp));

docs.title: screen_to_world(vec2 screen_px) → vec2
docs.text:
Convert a screen pixel position (top-left origin, y-down) to world space.

// Where did the user click in world space?
glyph::vec2 world = r.camera().screen_to_world(mouse_px);

docs.title: world_to_screen(vec2 world_pos) → vec2
docs.text:
Convert a world position to screen pixel coordinates.

// Is this entity visible?
glyph::vec2 screen = r.camera().world_to_screen(entity_pos);
bool on_screen = screen.x >= 0 && screen.x < 800
              && screen.y >= 0 && screen.y < 600;

docs.title: Coordinate system
docs.text:
World (y-down)          NDC / GL             Screen pixels
(0,0) top-left    →   (-1,+1) top-left  →   (0,0) top-left
  x → right              x → right              x → right
  y ↓ down               y ↑ up                 y ↓ down

The camera's view_projection() handles all three conversions automatically.

docs.title: Using with Renderer
docs.text:
void on_render(glyph::Renderer& r) override {
    glyph::Camera& cam = r.camera();
    cam.position = player_pos_;          // follow player
    cam.zoom     = 2.f;                  // zoomed in

    r.clear(glyph::Color::rgba8(20, 20, 30));
    // draw calls use cam's VP automatically
    r.draw_textured_quad(sprite_, dest_);
}

docs.title: FitMode (not yet implemented)
docs.text: enum class FitMode { Stretch, Letterbox, PixelPerfect } is declared but not yet wired into the renderer. Letterboxing / pillarboxing arrives alongside window resize handling.

docs.title: Game & Lifecycle
docs.text: Header: #include <glyph/app.h>

docs.title: AppConfig
docs.text:
Configuration returned by Game::configure(). The engine reads this before creating the window.

struct AppConfig {
    std::string title      = "Glyph Game";
    int         width      = 1280;
    int         height     = 720;
    bool        vsync      = true;
    bool        resizable  = true;
    bool        fullscreen = false;
};
Field Default Description title "Glyph Game" Window title bar text width 1280 Window width in logical pixels height 720 Window height in logical pixels vsync true Lock frame rate to display refresh resizable true Allow window resize fullscreen false Start in fullscreen mode

docs.title: Game
docs.text:
Base class for your game. Subclass it, override the hooks you need, and register with GLYPH_MAIN.

class Game {
public:
    virtual AppConfig configure();
    virtual void on_start();
    virtual void on_update(float dt);
    virtual void on_render(Renderer& r);
    virtual void on_shutdown();
};

docs.title: Lifecycle order
docs.text:
configure()          ← called before window creation
  ↓
[window + GL context created]
  ↓
on_start()           ← load assets, set up initial state
  ↓
loop:
    on_update(dt)    ← game logic, input (dt = seconds since last frame)
    on_render(r)     ← all draw calls go here
  ↓
on_shutdown()        ← clean up resources

docs.title: configure()
docs.text:
Called once before the window is created. Override to return a custom AppConfig.

glyph::AppConfig configure() override {
    glyph::AppConfig c;
    c.title = "Pong";
    c.width = 800;
    c.height = 600;
    return c;
}

docs.title: on_start()
docs.text:
Called once after the window, GL context, and engine subsystems are ready. Load textures and set up initial state here.

void on_start() override {
    my_texture_.create(/* ... */);
}

docs.title: on_update(float dt)
docs.text:
Called every frame. dt is the time in seconds since the last frame, capped to prevent spiral-of-death on slow frames.

void on_update(float dt) override {
    player_x_ += speed_ * dt;
}

docs.title: on_render(Renderer& r)
docs.text:
Called every frame after on_update. All draw calls must go here. The renderer is in a clean state at the start of each call.

void on_render(glyph::Renderer& r) override {
    r.clear(glyph::Color::rgba8(30, 30, 30));
    r.draw_textured_quad(my_texture_, {100, 100, 64, 64});
}

docs.title: on_shutdown()
docs.text: Called once before subsystems are torn down. Destroy GPU resources (textures, etc.) here.

docs.title: GLYPH_MAIN
docs.text:
Registers your Game subclass as the entry point. Call it at file scope in exactly one translation unit.

GLYPH_MAIN(MyGame)

This expands to:

extern "C" glyph::Game* glyph_create_game() { return new MyGame(); }

The engine's main() (in main_entry.cpp) resolves this symbol at link time.

Warning

GLYPH_MAIN must appear exactly once across your whole program. Putting it in a header that gets included multiple times will cause a linker error.

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
Header: #include <glyph/input.h>

Access via input() inside any Game lifecycle hook. The engine updates state from SDL events before each frame.

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
void on_update(float dt) override {
    // Axis — returns -1, 0, or +1 (clamped, normalised across all bound pairs)
    float vx = input().axis_value("move_x");   // A/D + Left/Right by default
    float vy = input().axis_value("move_y");   // W/S + Up/Down by default

    // Single-frame press detection
    if (input().action_pressed("confirm"))     // Space or Enter by default
        start_action();

    // Raw key query
    if (input().key_down(glyph::Key::LShift))
        speed *= 2.f;

    // Mouse
    glyph::vec2 cursor = input().mouse_position();
    float scroll = input().mouse_wheel().y;
}

docs.title: key_down(Key k) → bool
docs.text: True while the key is held.

docs.title: key_pressed(Key k) → bool
docs.text: True only on the frame the key went from up to down.

docs.title: key_released(Key k) → bool
docs.text: True only on the frame the key went from down to up.

docs.title: Key enum
docs.text:
Common values (full list in input.h):

Enum Key Key::A–Key::Z Letter keys Key::Num0–Key::Num9 Number row Key::Up/Down/Left/Right Arrow keys Key::Space, Key::Return, Key::Escape Special Key::LShift, Key::LCtrl, Key::LAlt Modifiers Key::F1–Key::F12 Function keys

docs.title: mouse_position() → vec2
docs.text: Current cursor position in screen pixels (top-left origin).

docs.title: mouse_down(MouseButton) → bool / mouse_pressed / mouse_released
docs.text: Same frame-delta semantics as keyboard.

docs.title: mouse_wheel() → vec2
docs.text: Accumulated scroll delta this frame. y > 0 = scroll up. Resets to zero each frame.

docs.title: MouseButton enum
docs.text: MouseButton::Left, MouseButton::Right, MouseButton::Middle

docs.title: Action bindings
docs.text: Named bindings decouple game logic from specific keys.

docs.title: bind_action(name, Key)
docs.text:
Add a key trigger to a named action. Multiple keys can be bound; any one fires the action.

input().bind_action("jump", glyph::Key::Space);
input().bind_action("jump", glyph::Key::Up);    // second binding for same action

docs.title: bind_axis(name, Key neg, Key pos)
docs.text:
Add a key pair to a named axis. Multiple pairs are OR'd and clamped to [-1, 1].

input().bind_axis("move_x", glyph::Key::Left, glyph::Key::Right);
input().bind_axis("move_x", glyph::Key::A,    glyph::Key::D);   // both pairs active

docs.title: action_down(name) / action_pressed(name) → bool
docs.text: action_pressed returns true only on the frame any bound key is first pressed.

docs.title: axis_value(name) → float
docs.text: Returns −1, 0, or +1 (clamped). Diagonal movement should be normalised by the caller.

docs.title: Default bindings
docs.text:
Set up in Input::Input(). Games can add more or override with bind_axis/bind_action.

Name Keys "move_x" A/D + Left/Right "move_y" W/S + Up/Down "confirm" Space, Return "cancel" Escape

docs.title: Frame timing
docs.text:
SDL events fired  →  SDL_AppEvent updates Input state
         ↓
SDL_AppIterate:  on_update() + on_render()  ←  game reads Input here
         ↓
Input::begin_frame()  — copies current→previous, resets mouse_wheel

key_pressed is true for exactly one frame per physical key press.

docs.title: Math Types
docs.text:
Header: #include <glyph/math.h>

Core math types and utilities. vec2, vec3, mat4, etc. are aliases over glm so game code can use the full glm API while staying in the glyph namespace.

docs.title: Type aliases
docs.text:
using vec2  = glm::vec2;
using vec3  = glm::vec3;
using vec4  = glm::vec4;
using mat3  = glm::mat3;
using mat4  = glm::mat4;
using ivec2 = glm::ivec2;

Because these are glm aliases, all glm operations work directly:

glyph::vec2 a{100.f, 200.f};
glyph::vec2 b{300.f, 400.f};

float  dist = glm::length(b - a);
glyph::vec2 norm = glm::normalize(b - a);
glyph::mat4 rot  = glm::rotate(glm::mat4(1.f), 0.5f, glyph::vec3(0, 0, 1));

docs.title: Color
docs.text:
Linear-space RGBA color. Components are float in [0.0, 1.0].

struct Color {
    float r = 1.f, g = 1.f, b = 1.f, a = 1.f;

    static Color rgba8(uint8_t r, uint8_t g, uint8_t b, uint8_t a = 255);
};

docs.title: Color::rgba8(r, g, b, a)
docs.text:
Convert 8-bit values (0–255) to a Color. The a parameter defaults to 255 (fully opaque).

glyph::Color red       = glyph::Color::rgba8(255,   0,   0);
glyph::Color sky_blue  = glyph::Color::rgba8(100, 149, 237);
glyph::Color half_alpha= glyph::Color::rgba8(255, 255, 255, 128);

docs.title: Direct construction
docs.text:
glyph::Color white{};              // {1, 1, 1, 1} — default
glyph::Color black{0, 0, 0, 1};
glyph::Color transparent{1,1,1,0};

docs.title: Rect
docs.text:
Axis-aligned rectangle in float coordinates (y-down, pixel units).

struct Rect {
    float x, y, w, h;

    bool contains(vec2 p) const;
    bool intersects(Rect o) const;
};
Field Description x, y Top-left corner w, h Width and height 
glyph::Rect screen{0.f, 0.f, 1280.f, 720.f};
glyph::Rect tile  {64.f, 32.f, 16.f, 16.f};

bool hit = tile.contains({70.f, 40.f});   // true
bool overlap = screen.intersects(tile);    // true

docs.title: ivec2
docs.text:
Integer 2D vector — an alias for glm::ivec2.

using ivec2 = glm::ivec2;

glyph::ivec2 drawable = window.drawable_size();  // {1920, 1080}
int area = drawable.x * drawable.y;

docs.title: lerp
docs.text:
float lerp(float a, float b, float t);
vec2  lerp(vec2  a, vec2  b, float t);

Linear interpolation. t = 0 returns a, t = 1 returns b.

float mid  = glyph::lerp(0.f, 100.f, 0.5f);   // 50.0
glyph::vec2 pos = glyph::lerp(start, end, 0.25f);

docs.title: Renderer
docs.text:
Header: #include <glyph/renderer.h>

The Renderer is passed to Game::on_render() each frame. All draw calls must happen inside that hook. The renderer is owned by the engine — do not store a pointer to it.

docs.title: Call order
docs.text:
Every frame, call in this order:

begin_frame()
  → clear()             (optional, but recommended)
  → draw_textured_quad() / [future draw calls]
end_frame()

The engine calls begin_frame() and end_frame() for you. Inside on_render(Renderer& r), you only need the draw calls.

docs.title: clear(Color c)
docs.text:
Fill the framebuffer with a solid color. Call this at the start of on_render to erase the previous frame.

void on_render(glyph::Renderer& r) override {
    r.clear(glyph::Color::rgba8(30, 30, 30));  // dark grey
}

docs.title: draw_textured_quad(const Texture& tex, Rect dest, Color tint)
docs.text:
Draw a single textured rectangle. dest is in pixel/screen coordinates (y-down, origin top-left).

Parameter Type Description tex const Texture& GPU texture to sample from dest Rect Destination rectangle {x, y, w, h} in screen pixels tint Color Multiply color. Default: {1,1,1,1} (no tint) 
// Draw a 64×64 quad at screen position (100, 100)
r.draw_textured_quad(my_texture_, {100.f, 100.f, 64.f, 64.f});

// Draw with a red tint
r.draw_textured_quad(my_texture_, {200.f, 100.f, 64.f, 64.f},
                     glyph::Color::rgba8(255, 100, 100));

No batching yet

Each draw_textured_quad call issues one GL draw call. The sprite batcher (phase 4) will collapse many draws into a single GPU submission.

docs.title: set_viewport(int w, int h)
docs.text: Update the GL viewport and recalculate the orthographic projection. Call this when the window is resized. The engine calls it automatically during init().

docs.title: Coordinate system
docs.text:
Glyph uses Y-down, pixel units:

  • (0, 0) is the top-left of the window.
  • X increases to the right; Y increases downward.
  • One unit = one screen pixel at zoom 1.0.
  • This matches SDL, Tiled, and most 2D conventions.
(0,0) ──────────────→ x
  │
  │
  ↓
  y

docs.title: Thread safety
docs.text: All Renderer methods must be called from the main thread. This is the only thread that may issue GL calls.

docs.title: Resources
docs.text:
Header: #include <glyph/resources.h>

Reference-counted asset cache. Assets are loaded on demand and cached by resolved path. The cache holds weak_ptr references — when all external shared_ptrs are released the cache entry expires and is cleaned up on the next unload_unused() call.

All methods must be called from the main thread.

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
class MyGame : public glyph::Game {
    glyph::Resources             res_;
    std::shared_ptr<glyph::Texture> player_;

    void on_start() override {
        res_.set_root("assets");
        player_ = res_.texture("player.png");
    }

    void on_render(glyph::Renderer& r) override {
        r.draw_textured_quad(*player_, {100, 100, 64, 64});
    }

    void on_shutdown() override {
        player_.reset();
        res_.unload_unused();
    }
};

docs.title: set_root(const std::string& path)
docs.text:
Set the directory prepended to all relative asset paths. Default: "assets".

res_.set_root("assets");          // looks in ./assets/
res_.set_root("/absolute/path");  // absolute path

docs.title: texture(const std::string& path) → shared_ptr<Texture>
docs.text:
Load a texture from an image file (PNG, JPG, BMP, TGA via stb_image). Returns a cached copy if the same path was loaded before and is still alive.

Returns nullptr and logs an error if the file cannot be read or decoded.

auto tex = res_.texture("tiles.png");
if (!tex) { /* handle error */ }

// Second call returns the same GPU texture — no re-upload.
auto tex2 = res_.texture("tiles.png");
assert(tex.get() == tex2.get());

docs.title: unload_unused()
docs.text:
Remove expired cache entries (those whose shared_ptrs have all been released). Call this in on_shutdown() or periodically when switching scenes.

void on_shutdown() override {
    player_.reset();
    res_.unload_unused();   // frees the GPU texture
}

docs.title: Path resolution
docs.text: Path Resolved to "player.png" {root}/player.png "ui/button.png" {root}/ui/button.png "/absolute/tex.png" /absolute/tex.png (unchanged) "C:\\data\\tex.png" C:\data\tex.png (unchanged)

docs.title: Lifetime
docs.text:
The Resources instance does not need to outlive the textures it created — each shared_ptr<Texture> is self-contained. However, keeping a single Resources per scene (or per game) avoids redundant disk reads.

GL context required

texture() uploads pixel data to the GPU. Only call it after on_start() while the GL context is active.

docs.title: Coming in future phases
docs.text: Method Phase sprite_sheet(path) 12 — Aseprite JSON tilemap(path) 13 — Tiled TMJ font(path, size) 15 — stb_truetype sound(path) 9 — miniaudio music(path) 9 — miniaudio

docs.title: Scene & Entity
docs.text:
Headers: #include <glyph/scene.h> · #include <glyph/components.h>

The ECS layer. EnTT drives the registry internally; Entity and Scene provide a friendlier façade. Access the scene via scene() inside any Game lifecycle hook.

docs.title: Entity
docs.text:
A value-typed handle: a Scene* plus an entt::entity id. Pass by value. Default-constructed entity is invalid.

glyph::Entity e = scene().create_entity("player");
e.add<glyph::Transform>(glyph::Transform{{400, 300}});
e.add<glyph::Sprite>(glyph::Sprite{res_.texture("player.png")});

// Retrieve
auto& t = e.get<glyph::Transform>();
t.position.x += 100.f;

// Query
if (e.has<glyph::Velocity>()) { /* ... */ }

// Optional (returns nullptr if missing)
if (auto* v = e.try_get<glyph::Velocity>()) v->value.x = 50.f;

// Remove
e.remove<glyph::Velocity>();

// Destroy
e.destroy();

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description valid() → bool True if the entity exists in the scene destroy() Remove the entity and all its components add<T>(args...) Attach component T (returns T&) get<T>() Get component T (UB if missing — use has first) try_get<T>() Get component T or nullptr has<T>() True if the entity has component T remove<T>() Detach component T

docs.title: Scene
docs.text:
void on_start() override {
    auto e = scene().create_entity("camera");
    e.add<glyph::Camera2D>();

    auto player = scene().create_entity("player");
    player.add<glyph::Transform>(glyph::Transform{{400, 300}});
    player.add<glyph::Velocity>();
}

void on_update(float dt) override {
    // Iterate all entities with Velocity and Transform
    scene().each<glyph::Velocity, glyph::Transform>(
        [](glyph::Entity e, glyph::Velocity& vel, glyph::Transform& xf) {
            xf.position += vel.value * dt;   // manual movement for now
        });
}

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description create_entity() Create an anonymous entity create_entity(name) Create entity with a Name component find(name) First entity with matching Name; invalid if not found each<Comps...>(fn) Iterate entities with all listed components run_systems(dt) Run built-in systems (Lifetime, Movement; others stubbed for later phases) render(r) Find primary Camera2D, sort sprites by layer, submit to batcher clear() Destroy all entities registry() Raw entt::registry& escape hatch

docs.title: Built-in components (components.h)
docs.text: Struct Fields Notes Name value: string Used by scene().find() Transform position, rotation, scale, parent Y-down, radians CW Sprite texture, src, tint, layer, origin, visible Rendered by Scene::render() Velocity value: vec2 World units per second; applied by MovementSystem each frame Animator sheet, clip, time, playing, loop Driven by AnimatorSystem (phase 12) BoxCollider bounds, layer, mask, is_trigger Used by CollisionSystem (phase 14) Camera2D value: Camera, primary: bool First primary used for rendering TilemapRef map: shared_ptr<Tilemap> Rendered by Scene (phase 13) Script lua_module: string Lua entity script; expanded in phase 16 Lifetime seconds: float Destroyed when ≤ 0 by LifetimeSystem each frame

docs.title: Transform hierarchy
docs.text: Set Transform::parent to another entity to form a parent-child relationship. TransformPropagationSystem is stubbed for now — correct implementation requires a separate world-transform store to avoid accumulating parent offsets each frame. Transform::position is always world-space until then. A null/invalid parent means no parent.

docs.title: EnTT escape hatch
docs.text:
For advanced use, the raw registry is accessible:

auto& reg = scene().registry();
auto view = reg.view<glyph::Transform, glyph::Sprite>();

docs.title: Sprite Sheets
docs.text:
Header: #include <glyph/sprite_sheet.h>

Loads Aseprite-exported sprite sheets (JSON + PNG). The Hash format is preferred; Array format is also supported.

docs.title: AnimClip
docs.text:
struct AnimClip {
    std::string        name;
    std::vector<int>   frame_indices;   // indices into SpriteSheet::frames
    std::vector<float> durations;       // seconds, parallel to frame_indices
    bool               loop = true;
};

docs.title: SpriteSheet
docs.text:
class SpriteSheet {
public:
    std::shared_ptr<Texture>                  texture;
    std::vector<Rect>                         frames;
    std::unordered_map<std::string, AnimClip> clips;

    static std::shared_ptr<SpriteSheet> load(const std::string& json_path);
};

load() parses the JSON sidecar and loads the companion PNG (resolved from meta.image relative to the JSON path). Returns nullptr on failure.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
Export from Aseprite with File → Export Sprite Sheet, choose JSON (Hash) output. Both the .json and the .png must be in the same directory.

void on_start() override {
    auto sheet = glyph::SpriteSheet::load("assets/player.json");

    auto e = scene().create_entity("player");
    e.add<glyph::Transform>(glyph::Transform{{200, 300}});
    e.add<glyph::Sprite>();           // src and texture set by AnimatorSystem
    auto& anim = e.add<glyph::Animator>();
    anim.sheet   = sheet;
    anim.clip    = "run";
    anim.playing = true;
}

AnimatorSystem (run automatically by Scene::run_systems) advances Animator::time each frame, resolves the current frame from the active clip, and writes Sprite::src and Sprite::texture.

docs.title: Animator component
docs.text:
Field Type Description sheet shared_ptr<SpriteSheet> The loaded sprite sheet clip string Active clip name (must match a key in sheet->clips) time float Playback time in seconds; reset to 0 when switching clips playing bool Pauses when false; set to false automatically at end of non-looping clip loop bool Overridden by AnimClip::loop — the clip's own loop flag is used

Note

Reset Animator::time to 0.f when switching clips.

docs.title: Texture
docs.text:
Header: #include <glyph/texture.h>

A 2D GPU texture. Created from raw RGBA8 pixel data. In phase 6, Resources::texture() will load from PNG/JPG files via stb_image — for now, create textures manually from pixel arrays.

docs.title: Creating a texture
docs.text:
// 2×2 checkerboard (black and white)
uint8_t pixels[2 * 2 * 4] = {
    255, 255, 255, 255,   0,   0,   0, 255,   // row 0: white, black
      0,   0,   0, 255, 255, 255, 255, 255,   // row 1: black, white
};

glyph::Texture tex;
tex.create(2, 2, pixels);

GL context required

create() uploads data to the GPU. Call it after on_start() — never in a constructor or before the engine initializes.

docs.title: create(int w, int h, const uint8_t* rgba_pixels) → bool
docs.text:
Upload RGBA8 pixels and allocate a GL texture object. Returns true on success.

  • Pixel format: 4 bytes per pixel, R G B A order, top-row first.
  • Filtering: nearest-neighbor (sharp pixels, no blurring — ideal for pixel art).
  • Wrap mode: CLAMP_TO_EDGE.
bool ok = tex.create(width, height, pixel_data);
if (!ok) { /* handle error */ }

docs.title: destroy()
docs.text:
Free the GPU texture. Call this in Game::on_shutdown() for any textures you created manually.

void on_shutdown() override {
    tex_.destroy();
}

Calling destroy() on an already-destroyed texture is safe (no-op).

docs.title: bind(int slot = 0)
docs.text: Bind to a GL texture slot (0–7). You generally don't need to call this directly — draw calls handle binding internally.

docs.title: valid() → bool
docs.text: Returns true if the texture has been successfully created and not yet destroyed.

docs.title: width() → int / height() → int
docs.text: Dimensions in pixels.

docs.title: id() → unsigned int
docs.text: The raw GL texture name. Exposed for advanced use; prefer the higher-level draw API.

docs.title: Lifetime
docs.text:
Texture is a plain value type — it doesn't auto-destroy when it goes out of scope. Always pair create() with a matching destroy() call in on_shutdown().

class MyGame : public glyph::Game {
    glyph::Texture atlas_;

    void on_start() override    { atlas_.create(/* ... */); }
    void on_shutdown() override { atlas_.destroy(); }
};

Future: Resources

Phase 6 introduces Resources::texture(path) which returns a shared_ptr<Texture> with automatic lifetime management and caching.

docs.title: Time
docs.text:
Header: #include <glyph/time.h>

Frame timing and the fixed-timestep accumulator. Access via time() inside any Game lifecycle hook.

Implements the "Fix Your Timestep" pattern (Glenn Fiedler, 2006): physics and gameplay logic run at a fixed 60 Hz regardless of display frame rate.

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
void on_fixed_update(float dt) override {
    // dt is always Time::kFixedDt (1/60 s)
    // Put physics, AI, and frame-rate-sensitive logic here
    velocity_ += gravity_ * dt;
    position_ += velocity_ * dt;
}

void on_update(float dt) override {
    // dt is variable (capped, time-scaled)
    // Use for input reading, camera, and anything tolerant of variable rate
}

void on_render(glyph::Renderer& r) override {
    // Interpolate between physics frames for smooth rendering
    float alpha = time().fixed_alpha();
    glyph::vec2 render_pos = glyph::lerp(prev_pos_, position_, alpha);
    r.draw_textured_quad(sprite_, {render_pos.x, render_pos.y, 32, 32});
}

docs.title: Constants
docs.text:
Constant Value Description Time::kFixedDt 1/60 ≈ 0.01667 s Fixed update step duration Time::kMaxAccum 0.25 s Accumulator cap — prevents spiral of death

If a frame takes longer than kMaxAccum, at most 0.25 / (1/60) = 15 fixed steps run that frame. The simulation slows down rather than running away.

docs.title: delta() → float
docs.text: Time-scaled, capped frame delta in seconds. Always use this in on_update, not raw dt.

docs.title: elapsed() → double
docs.text: Unscaled wall-clock seconds since the engine started.

docs.title: frame_count() → uint64_t
docs.text: Total number of frames rendered.

docs.title: fps() → float
docs.text: Exponentially smoothed frame rate (~20-frame window). Suitable for HUD display.

docs.title: fixed_alpha() → float
docs.text:
Sub-step interpolation factor in [0, 1]. How far into the next fixed step the current render is: - 0.0 = rendering at exactly the previous fixed-update state - 1.0 = rendering at exactly the current fixed-update state

Use with lerp() to eliminate visual jitter when on_fixed_update runs slower than the display.

docs.title: set_time_scale(float s)
docs.text: Scale all time. 1.0 = normal, 0.5 = half speed (slow motion), 2.0 = double speed. Affects delta() and the fixed-step accumulator.

docs.title: Frame loop sequence
docs.text:
time.tick(raw_dt)           — cap, scale, accumulate
while time.step_fixed():    — drain fixed budget (max kMaxAccum / kFixedDt per frame)
    on_fixed_update(1/60)
on_update(time.delta())     — variable-rate
on_render(renderer)         — use fixed_alpha() for interpolation

docs.title: Slow-frame verification
docs.text: With kMaxAccum = 0.25 s, a 2-second freeze causes exactly 15 fixed steps on the next frame, not 120. The game slows to 1/8 speed temporarily instead of running ahead to catch up.

docs.title: C++ Conventions
docs.text: Rules that apply to every file in the engine. These exist so the codebase stays consistent as it grows across 23 phases.

docs.title: Language and compatibility
docs.text:
• C++17. No C++20 features — needed for Emscripten, Android NDK, and Xcode toolchains.
  • Exceptions on — sol2 and EnTT use them.
  • RTTI on — EnTT and sol2 require it.

docs.title: Naming
docs.text:
Kind Style Example Types (class, struct, enum) PascalCase SpriteBatch, Entity Functions and methods snake_case draw_sprite, screen_to_world Variables, parameters snake_case tile_count, cam_pos Member variables snake_case_ (trailing _) texture_, frame_count_ Constants kPascalCase kMaxBatchSize Macros SCREAMING_SNAKE GLYPH_MAIN Namespaces lower glyph, glyph::detail Files snake_case sprite_batch.cpp

No Hungarian notation. No m_ prefixes.

docs.title: File layout
docs.text:
• include/glyph/*.h — public API. What game developers include.
  • src/<subsystem>/*.{h,cpp} — implementation and internal headers. Never #include'd from public headers.
  • One primary type per file. Filename matches the type.
  • #pragma once for header guards.

docs.title: Include order (in .cpp files)
docs.text:
1. Corresponding public header (#include <glyph/renderer.h>)
  2. Other engine internal headers
  3. Third-party headers (SDL, glm, etc.)
  4. C++ standard library

docs.title: Memory
docs.text:
• Owning pointers → std::unique_ptr<T> or std::shared_ptr<T>.
  • Non-owning → references (T&) or raw pointers (T*) when null is meaningful.
  • Never new/delete outside RAII wrappers.
  • Entity is a value handle — pass by value.

docs.title: Comments
docs.text:
Comments explain why, not what. The code shows what.

// SDL_FunctionPointer → void* via memcpy: function and data pointers are the
// same size on all our targets; this is the pedantically-correct conversion.

Not:

// bind the texture to slot 0
tex.bind(0);   // ← don't do this

docs.title: Platform isolation
docs.text: #ifdef GLYPH_PLATFORM_* guards are only allowed inside src/platform/. Everywhere else uses the platform-agnostic API.

docs.title: Error handling
docs.text: Situation Strategy Engine init failure throw std::runtime_error(...) — caught by main loop, logged, app exits Missing asset Log + return fallback (magenta texture, empty tilemap) Lua error Caught by sol2 protected_function, logged with stack trace Debug invariant GLYPH_ASSERT(condition, message) — stripped in release

docs.title: Build Progress
docs.text: Tracks position in the 23-step implementation order from the engine spec.

docs.title: Completed phases
docs.text: Phase Description Tag ✅ 1 CMake skeleton + SDL3 window — 01_hello_window opens cleanly v0.1.0 ✅ 2 GL context + clear color — glad2 loaded, vsync swap v0.2.0 ✅ 3 Shader + textured quad — sprite shader, draw_textured_quad v0.3.0 ✅ 4 Sprite batcher — 2000-quad buffer, 8-texture slots, auto-flush v0.4.0 ✅ 5 Camera + math — glm aliases, Camera::view_projection, pan/zoom v0.5.0 ✅ 6 Resources + Texture loading — stb_image, Resources cache, 02_sprite v0.6.0 ✅ 7 Input system + action mapping — Key/MouseButton, axes, 03_input v0.7.0 ✅ 8 Time + fixed timestep — on_fixed_update, fixed_alpha, smoothed FPS v0.8.0 ✅ 9 Audio — miniaudio, Sound/Music, SoundHandle, volume, fade v0.9.0 ✅ 10 EnTT + Entity façade — Scene, Entity, components.h v0.10.0 ✅ 11 Built-in systems — MovementSystem, LifetimeSystem, Scene::render() with sprite sorting v0.11.0 ✅ 12 Aseprite JSON loader — SpriteSheet::load(), AnimClip, AnimatorSystem v0.12.0

docs.title: Current phase
docs.text: Phase 13: Tiled TMJ loader + tilemap renderer — samples/04_tilemap.
